design_authority.kernel: Application Kernel owns operation definitions, request normalization, policy decisions, journal transitions, recovery, and Agent result normalization.
design_authority.resource_truth: Existing registry, project-local runtime state, global run index, ownership proofs, process/port adapters, logs, and events remain the resource-state authorities; the operation journal references rather than replaces them.
design_authority.shared_state: Every persisted record is rooted under the same resolved LAUNCHDECK_HOME for CLI, MCP runtime, and both Plugin packages. No Plugin-local registry, journal, lock, or run index is permitted.
design_authority.basis: Locked by spec-contract constraints, D-001, D-007, D-010, CAP-001..CAP-004, and evidenced by src/control-plane/state.js and src/control-plane/runs.js.

live_evidence.claim: LAUNCHDECK_HOME already owns the versioned registry, runtime run index, indexes, locks, events, and logs namespaces, so the operation journal belongs in this user-scoped tree rather than a Plugin directory.

live_evidence.span: readRegistryState/writeRegistryState, lines 49-83; validation, lines 209-252; atomicWriteJson, lines 286-290
live_evidence.claim: Current state practice is explicit schema versioning, fail-closed reads for unsupported versions, validation before writes, stable identity, and temp-file-plus-rename atomic replacement.
Current migration practice normalizes legacy state in memory and records migratedFrom metadata before a later mutation persists the current schema.
The global run index is versioned; active run states are starting/running/ready/stopping; start is protected by project then task locks and reuses an existing managed process before spawning.
Runs already carry stable project identity, runId, transactionId, state-home provenance, process identity, status, declared ports, log path, and ownership evidence.
Repeated start returns the existing run metadata with spawned=false and records the same run rather than creating another process.
An active run whose recorded owned PID is no longer alive maps to stale while retaining ownership proof and explicit stale evidence.
Current asynchronous mutations use project, task, and run-index locks plus atomic JSON replacement.

live_evidence.span: constants and acquireLock, lines 5-58; lock record and release, lines 80-123; stale/takeover checks, lines 171-198
live_evidence.claim: Locks are exclusive files with owner PID, transaction, TTL, bounded wait, owner-aware stale takeover, and release guarded by exact owner identity.
Ownership confidence is verified-owned, probable-owned, stale-owned, external, or unknown and is derived from run, listener PID, liveness, cwd, command, environment marker, and start-time evidence.
Only verified-owned observations expose stop; unknown or external ownership is inspect-only and blocks stop, force-stop, and restart.
Persisted spawn proof is accepted only when run, project, task, PID, start time, command, cwd, transaction, and project root identities agree.

live_evidence.span: readState/writeState and process refresh, lines 62-110; validateRuntimeState, lines 972-1025
live_evidence.claim: Project-local runtime state is schemaVersion 1, bound to an exact project root, and validates process record fields and lifecycle status before use.
Stop transitions running to stopping then stopped or stop_failed, persists both local and global evidence, and refuses when ownership is external, unknown, stale, or otherwise unverified.
Clean is already plan-then-apply at the target level, refuses the whole mutation when any target is unsafe, and preserves per-target missing/removed/partially_removed evidence.
Configured project resolution already accepts stable ID, alias, name, basename, or exact root and refuses missing and ambiguous matches.
Reconciliation is scope-bounded, repairs stale evidence only when runId or PID matches, and leaves live or insufficiently proven runs unresolved.

live_evidence.span: appendEvent/readEvents, lines 13-74; event record and limits, lines 85-121; redaction, lines 124-166
live_evidence.claim: Events are versioned JSONL with transaction/run correlation, bounded default 200 and maximum 1000 reads, malformed-line diagnostics, and persistence-time secret redaction.

live_evidence.span: RISK_LEVELS and normalizeTask, lines 13 and 110-156; normalizeClean, lines 171-220
live_evidence.claim: Configured task risk is one of low/medium/high/destructive and commands, cwd, env, ports, lifecycle kind, and safe/risky clean declarations come from validated project configuration.

live_evidence.span: test 'start project:task returns the existing run without spawning a second process', lines 353-368
live_evidence.claim: Existing verification proves repeat-start runId/PID reuse and a single spawn.
Registry creation, legacy migration, stable project identity, alias collision, unsupported-version refusal, and repair are current verification owners.
Exclusive acquisition, bounded contention, live-owner refusal, dead-owner takeover, and release on success/error are verified lock behaviors.
Ownership verification requires corroboration; mismatched spawn proof cannot upgrade unknown evidence; unknown ownership stays visible as unknown.
Clean refusal is all-or-nothing for unsafe targets and preserves running/latest-failure evidence; reset remains absent.

live_evidence.span: canonical target and idempotency tests, lines 7-27 and 103-138; divergent target test, lines 140-181
live_evidence.claim: A canonical Skill source, multi-host targets, idempotent identical installs, and divergent-target refusal already exist; the forced overwrite branch is a contract gap to constrain for the new non-destructive duplicate policy.

entities.basis: Spec-owned: D-001, CAP-001, public_operation_contract; phase-owned placement recommendation.

fields.name: Stable canonical ID such as task.start or operation.reconcile.
fields.definitionVersion: Positive integer version for the operation contract.
fields.inputSchemaRef: Single source schema consumed by Kernel validation and MCP tool generation/validation.
fields.allowedSurfaces: cli, mcp, skill-routing-internal, or diagnostic-only set.
fields.projectScopePolicy: configured-project requirement and permitted resolver sources.
fields.riskEvaluator: Kernel function that evaluates current configured risk at execution time.
fields.compatibilityRequirement: Required protocol/state/schema/component axes for observation and mutation.
fields.lockScopes: Ordered lock key templates, normally operation then project then task then run-index.
fields.journalPolicy: none for pure observations or required-before-effects for mutations.
fields.effectModel: Declared possible resource/effect classes used for reconciliation.
fields.handlerRef: Kernel-owned executor; adapters never own alternate handlers.
fields.resultMapperRef: Normalizer to AgentOperationResult plus CLI schemaVersion 1 mapping.
fields.deprecatedAliases: Optional CLI compatibility aliases that resolve to this definition without changing canonical identity.

invariants: Operation names are unique and immutable within an Agent protocol major version.
Public MCP inputs are a strict projection of the Kernel schema and never add command, shell, env, cwd, force, yes, confirmed, approvalToken, or arbitraryPath.
Risk annotations in MCP or host manifests are descriptive only; riskEvaluator is authoritative.
A mutation cannot be registered without required-before-effects journaling, explicit effectModel, lock scopes, and reconciliation behavior.

entities.owner: Surface adapter for transport decoding; Kernel after schema validation
entities.basis: Spec-owned by public_operation_contract and CA-002/CA-008/CA-017; canonical digest rules are phase-owned.

fields.projectRef: Optional declared project stable ID or alias.
fields.explicitProjectRoot: Optional typed root hint that must resolve to a registered project; never an arbitrary execution path.
fields.hostProjectRoot: Optional host-reported workspace root, treated as untrusted resolution evidence.
fields.recoveryOperationId: Optional previously Kernel-issued ID, valid only for get/reconcile/recovery of original work.
fields.requestId: Transport correlation ID; not the operation identity.

invariants: Adapters do not synthesize approvals or relax the input schema.
A recoveryOperationId never nominates a new operation ID.
Conflicting project references are a scope refusal rather than precedence-based guessing.

The normalized input digest excludes transport requestId and presentation metadata but includes canonical operation name, validated input, resolved projectId/taskRef, relevant config/task-definition digest, and supplied clean plan digest.

entities.basis: Spec-owned by CAP-002..CAP-004 and result_contract provenance; existing run context evidenced at src/control-plane/runs.js:67-80.

fields.operationId: Kernel-issued immutable op_<base64url> for new mutations, or recovered known ID.
fields.inputDigest: sha256 over canonical versioned semantic input.
fields.configSnapshot: Config version/path/hash and selected task-definition digest; no unredacted secrets in journal projection.
fields.ownershipEvidence: OwnershipEvidence when the operation can affect a run/process/port.
fields.compatibility: Compatibility evaluation and exact ComponentIdentity set.
fields.runtimeProvenance: Surface, host, runtime kind/version/path, state home, build identity, protocol/schema versions.
fields.clock: Request/prepared/running timestamps from one Kernel clock source.

invariants: Context is derived, not caller-authored.
Resolved stateHome must be identical to the registry/run/journal state root used by the executor.
Policy-sensitive fields are re-evaluated after locks are held and before the running transition/first effect.
Journal projection is redacted and bounded; raw command/env may remain only in existing protected runtime/config authorities, not the public Agent result.

entities.basis: Specified exactly by D-006 and spec-contract.result_contract.

fields.protocolVersion: Agent protocol version independent from CLI schemaVersion.
fields.outcome: {kind,code,message,reusedExistingRun}; kind is succeeded, failed, partial, refused, or indeterminate.
fields.resource: {kind,id,status,projectRef,taskRef,runId}; lifecycle status is independent from outcome.kind.
fields.nextActions: Typed safe actions; never smuggles forbidden raw controls.

invariants: All nine top-level fields are present; nullable fields use null rather than disappearing where schemas require presence.
outcome.kind never substitutes for resource.status.
A reused active run is outcome succeeded, resource running or ready, effects certainty confirmed, effects.changed false, and reusedExistingRun true.
An indeterminate outcome has effects certainty possible or unknown and only operation.get/reconcile next actions.
The CLI adapter preserves schemaVersion 1 field meaning and may nest/map this result without replacing the public CLI envelope.

entities.owner: Application Kernel operation handler and reconciler

fields.changed: Boolean when known; null when certainty is unknown.
fields.effectKinds: Bounded declared effect classes such as process_spawned, process_stopped, files_removed, registry_unchanged.
fields.evidenceRefs: Stable references to journal snapshots, run records, ownership proof, events, or clean target results.
fields.reconciliationRequired: True for possible or unknown mutation effects.

invariants: none means evidence proves no external mutation began.
confirmed means evidence proves the reported change or no-change; it does not imply every intended effect succeeded.
possible means at least one declared effect may have occurred but final state is incomplete; unknown means available evidence cannot bound occurrence.
possible/unknown is never automatically replayed through either surface.

entities.basis: Specified by D-007, journal_contract, AC-006/AC-007, and CA-005/CA-011/CA-026..CA-029/CA-042..CA-044; physical layout is phase-owned.

fields.operationId: Immutable Kernel-issued ID and primary key.
fields.definitionVersion: Operation contract version used for execution.
fields.requestSummary: Bounded redacted summary sufficient for diagnosis and lost-response correlation.
fields.projectRef: Resolved stable projectId plus display alias snapshot.
fields.state: prepared, running, succeeded, failed, refused, indeterminate, or reconciled.
fields.resourceRef: Resource kind/id/runId without duplicating mutable resource truth.
fields.resultRef: Inline bounded terminal AgentOperationResult snapshot or relative immutable result blob ref.
fields.resolvedOutcome: Required when state is reconciled; succeeded, failed, partial, or refused.
fields.retainUntil: terminalAt plus default 30 days for terminal full records; null for unresolved records.
fields.revision: Monotonic record revision used for compare-and-update diagnostics.

invariants: A mutation record reaches durable prepared before project/task side effects.
operationId, operationName, definitionVersion, inputDigest, projectRef, taskRef, createdAt, and original runtimeProvenance never change.
Same operationId plus same digest returns current/original work; it never executes a second handler.
Same operationId plus a different digest returns operation_id_digest_mismatch without changing the record or starting work.
A supplied unknown, pruned, or expired recovery ID returns operation_record_missing_or_expired and cannot become a new record.
prepared, running, and indeterminate have retainUntil null and are not age-pruned.
Journal state does not overwrite run/resource status; it records operation progress and links to resource evidence.

entities.owner: Application Kernel project resolver over existing registry
entities.basis: Spec-owned by CAP-002, AC-009, CA-009/CA-020; registered resolver behavior evidenced at src/control-plane/actions.js:303-330. Precedence details are phase-owned.

fields.status: resolved, missing, ambiguous, conflicting, or scope_violation.
fields.source: project_ref, explicit_root, host_root, cli_local_config, or none.
fields.projectId: Stable registered project ID when resolved.
fields.projectRoot: Canonical registered root, never Plugin installation cwd.
fields.candidates: Bounded candidate summaries for ambiguous/conflicting results.
fields.evidence: Normalized supplied refs and exact registry match reasons.

invariants: Public MCP mutations resolve only to exactly one configured registry project.
projectRef has no silent fallback: all supplied hints must resolve to the same project or the request is conflicting.
A sole explicit/host root must exactly match one registered canonical root for MCP; it never authorizes an unregistered project.
No hint yields missing unless a CLI-local compatibility path explicitly resolves the current project by existing CLI rules.
Plugin package/install directories are excluded as default project roots.

entities.owner: Existing control-plane ownership service; Kernel consumes its decision
entities.basis: Live-evidenced at src/control-plane/ownership.js:8-197 and 446-473; mutation rule locked by AC-004/CA-004/CA-021/CA-031.

fields.confidence: verified-owned, probable-owned, stale-owned, external, or unknown.
fields.source: launchdeck-spawn, live-process, port-listener, liveness, or composite.
fields.portEvidence: Bounded listener PID/source evidence for declared ports.
fields.trustedSpawnProof: Persisted immutable spawn proof when exact identity checks pass.

invariants: PID or port alone never proves Launchdeck ownership.
A listener PID mismatch is external; incomplete listener/process evidence is unknown.
Only verified-owned permits stop/restart or other process mutation.
probable-owned, stale-owned, external, and unknown remain inspect/reconcile only.
No operation, Plugin lifecycle, clean, rollback, or fallback path terminates an external or unknown-owned process.

entities.basis: Spec-owned by D-003, CAP-003, AC-005, CA-007/CA-008/CA-017; risk vocabulary live-evidenced at src/config.js:13,110-156.

fields.configuredRisk: low, medium, high, or destructive from current validated task/config.
fields.effectiveRisk: Kernel-evaluated risk after operation, resource, plan, and current-state modifiers.
fields.reasonCodes: Stable reasons including risk_not_low, scope_not_resolved, ownership_not_verified, compatibility_mismatch, plan_digest_stale, lock_busy.

invariants: First-release public MCP mutation allows only current effectiveRisk low with resolved configured project, compatible components, required ownership, and acquired locks.
Host approvals, MCP annotations, and model-authored confirmation fields cannot change decision.
Medium/high/destructive MCP execution is refused even if the CLI has an existing interactive path.
Policy is recomputed after relevant locks are acquired and before the first side effect.

entities.owner: Build/release source manifest; Kernel compatibility evaluator consumes generated runtime copy
entities.basis: Spec-owned by D-002/D-005/D-010, AC-010/AC-011, CA-010/CA-025/CA-032..CA-035; schema shape is phase-owned.

fields.buildIdentity: One immutable build ID shared by generated artifacts.
fields.stateSchemas: Registry, run-index, project-runtime, journal, lock, and event readable/writable ranges.
fields.componentDigests: Canonical runtime bundle, operation schemas, Skill content manifest, and host manifest digests.
fields.supportedOperations: Canonical operation IDs plus definition versions and public surface/risk limits.
fields.hostArtifacts: Separate Codex and Claude package identities generated from this manifest.
fields.migrationCapabilities: Explicit readable, writable, and diagnostic-only version edges.

invariants: Package, Agent protocol, CLI schema, state schema, operation schema, Skill digest, runtime digest, and host artifact identity are separate axes.
Mutation requires readable-and-writable state compatibility plus matching required runtime/schema/build digests; mismatch fails writes closed.
Bounded capabilities/diagnosis may run in diagnostic-only mode on a mismatch and must report exact axes.
Codex and Claude artifacts have distinct host identities but the same buildIdentity and canonical component digests.

entities.owner: Each CLI/MCP/Skill/Plugin component; verified by Kernel capabilities
entities.basis: Spec-owned by result provenance, compatibility constraints, and CA-010/CA-035/CA-037/CA-038.

fields.kind: cli, mcp-runtime, canonical-skill, codex-plugin, or claude-plugin.
fields.manifestDigest: Digest of the manifest consumed at build/install.
fields.runtimeDigest: Bundled/runtime executable content digest where applicable.
fields.skillDigest: Canonical Skill content-manifest digest where applicable.
fields.installedPath: Observed absolute component path for provenance only, never policy authority.

invariants: A path match does not establish component compatibility; digests and version ranges do.
Compatible newer CLI/Plugin may control an older run only when state schema, operation semantics, and ownership proof pass.
Host exit/update/disable/uninstall does not own managed-task lifetime or shared-state lifetime.

entities.basis: Specified by D-008, skill_migration_contract, AC-013, CA-023/CA-047/CA-048; current installer evidence at test/agent-installer.test.js:7-181.

fields.locationKind: codex-current-user, codex-legacy-user, codex-project, claude-project, plugin-bundled-codex, or plugin-bundled-claude.
fields.state: absent, single, duplicate-identical, duplicate-divergent, invalid-target, or unreadable.
fields.digestGroups: Locations grouped by canonical content-manifest digest.
fields.selectedLocation: Deterministic host-preferred active location only when absent/single/identical rules permit.
fields.recommendedActions: User-controlled install, migrate, select, or cleanup instructions.

invariants: Codex scans both $HOME/.agents/skills/launchdeck-agent and ~/.codex/skills/launchdeck-agent.
Canonical digest is sha256 over a sorted versioned manifest of relative path, file type, size, and per-file content digest; arbitrary absolute paths and mtimes are excluded.
Identical duplicates are reported, current Codex location wins deterministic precedence, and cleanup is only suggested.
Divergent duplicates preserve every file, expose every digest, select no silent winner, and require explicit user-selected migration/removal before single-active readiness.
New Codex user installation targets only $HOME/.agents/skills; an identical target is idempotent; divergent target content is not overwritten by automatic install/update.
Plugin-bundled copies remain version-coupled to their bundled runtime and are never silently treated as the user installation.

entities.basis: Spec-owned by CAP-009, AC-008, CA-013/CA-030; existing target planning evidenced at src/runtime.js:357-438. Digest envelope is phase-owned.

fields.planId: Diagnostic plan identity; not execution authority by itself.
fields.projectRootDigest: Digest of canonical root identity, never the raw root as public authority.
fields.mode: safe only for public Agent apply.
fields.refusals: Unsafe/ambiguous target codes; any entry blocks apply.
fields.planDigest: sha256 of versioned canonical projectId/configHash/mode/target/refusal payload.
fields.effectsPreview: Bounded expected removals/skips and evidence refs.

invariants: clean.plan performs no deletion.

clean.applySafe accepts only planDigest plus declared project reference, creates a mutation journal record, acquires operation/project locks, recomputes a fresh plan, and compares exact digest before effects.
Any config, target, canonical path, protected evidence, existence/type, or refusal drift produces plan_digest_stale with effects none/changed false.
Only targets still classified safe are deleted; risky, root, outside-root, symlink escape, empty, or ambiguous targets are refused.
Safe clean never removes shared LAUNCHDECK_HOME or evidence needed to identify active/current failed runs.

entities.basis: Spec-owned by CAP-007, public_operation_contract.observation_boundary, CA-036; limits reuse src/control-plane/events.js:5-7,105-121 and src/runtime.js:335-354. Cursor token design is phase-owned.

fields.resourceId: Resolved project/task or journal namespace identity; no caller path.
fields.snapshotHighWater: File length or createdAt/operationId tuple fixed for this paging session.
fields.expiresAt: Bounded cursor validity; cursor expiry never affects underlying journal retention.
fields.token: base64url canonical payload plus integrity digest; treated as opaque by callers.

invariants: The cursor cannot select an arbitrary path and is rejected if kind/resource/query digest changes.
Each call is bounded: events default 200/max 1000 records; logs default 80/max 1000 lines and max 256 KiB response; operation.list default 50/max 200 records.
snapshotHighWater prevents one page sequence from becoming permanent follow; end-of-snapshot returns nextCursor null.
A new call is required to observe later data.
Secret redaction occurs before persistence for events and before response projection for logs/journal summaries.

entities.basis: Locked by D-010, AC-014/AC-015, CA-039/CA-040/CA-046/CA-049/CA-050; physical artifact format is phase-owned.

fields.cellId: Stable digest/key over exact readiness dimensions.
fields.surface: operation-contract, cli, stdio-mcp, skill, codex-host, claude-host, update, uninstall, recovery, or platform.
fields.host: standalone, codex version, or claude version.
fields.scenario: Positive, refusal, interruption, restart, concurrency, host-exit, update, disable, uninstall, takeover, or recovery.
fields.evidenceRefs: Test IDs, artifact paths, command receipts, and captured build/component identities.
fields.expiresOrRevalidateOn: Build, host, OS, schema, or manifest changes that invalidate the cell.
fields.dependsOn: Other exact cells required for a composed claim.

invariants: A passed cell proves only its exact build/surface/operation/host/OS/scenario dimensions.
Failed, blocked, unsupported, missing, or stale cells withhold that claim and every aggregate depending on it.
Codex evidence never proves Claude, one OS never proves another, and install never proves update/uninstall/recovery.
Every readiness claim exposes evidence refs and compatibility identity.

relationships.rule: The definition validates and normalizes every request; adapters cannot fork semantics.
Kernel resolves project, config, risk, ownership, compatibility, digest, operation ID, and lock keys.
Every mutation has one durable journal record before effects; pure observations may be not-journaled but still return an ephemeral invocation operation ID.
The journal persists/refers to the normalized result; same-ID recovery returns it without re-execution.

relationships.cardinality: N:0..1 primary resource plus N evidence refs
relationships.rule: resourceRef/runId links existing run/ownership/event/clean evidence; mutable resource state is never duplicated as a second authority.
Missing, ambiguous, conflicting, or out-of-scope resolution forces refusal.
Process-affecting mutations require verified-owned; start additionally requires declared ports free or attributable to the same reused run.
Capabilities compares each observed component against independently versioned axes and digests.
Each discovered user or Plugin Skill copy reports digest/build coupling and host location separately.
Preview is read-only; applySafe journals a new mutation and binds/revalidates planDigest before deletion.
Cursor pages only a bounded high-water snapshot of an already resolved source.
Evidence is invalidated when its tested build/manifest/runtime/schema/Skill identities change.

operation_journal.basis: Spec-contract.journal_contract; exact transition guards are phase-owned design.

transitions.guard: Definition/input/scope are syntactically valid and a new Kernel operation ID is allocated.
transitions.actions: Persist immutable identity/digest/context summary with effectsCertainty none before effects.

transitions.guard: Operation lock plus declared resource locks are held; execution-time risk, scope, ownership, compatibility, and optional plan digest all pass.
transitions.actions: Persist running/revision/startedAt before first declared side effect.

transitions.guard: Any execution-time policy/scope/ownership/compatibility/lock/plan precondition refuses before effects.
transitions.actions: Persist terminal refusal with effects none, changed false, and safe next actions.

transitions.guard: A non-policy internal failure is proven to occur before effects.
transitions.actions: Persist terminal failure with effects none and diagnostic evidence.

transitions.guard: Declared postconditions/evidence confirm the intended result or confirmed idempotent no-change.
transitions.actions: Persist terminal result; effects confirmed; resource status remains separately mapped.

transitions.guard: Failure and final effects are bounded by evidence.
transitions.actions: Persist outcome failed or partial with effects none/confirmed/possible as justified; possible additionally requires reconcile next action.

transitions.guard: Transport/process interruption or persistence failure prevents a defensible final effect determination.
transitions.actions: Persist possible or unknown effects when possible, emit recovery evidence, never retry automatically.

transitions.guard: Explicit operation.reconcile after crash proves no effect or a final resource outcome.
transitions.actions: Persist resolvedOutcome, evidence refs, terminalAt, and retention horizon without executing original work.

transitions.guard: Explicit reconciliation after abandoned executor proves a final resource outcome.
transitions.actions: Persist resolvedOutcome/effects from live and durable evidence; no replay.

transitions.guard: Explicit reconciliation obtains sufficient run/process/port/file evidence.
transitions.actions: Persist resolvedOutcome succeeded/failed/partial/refused and confirmed/none effects; if evidence remains insufficient, stay indeterminate.

prohibited: Any terminal state back to running
Same operation ID creating a second prepared record
Recovery of a missing/expired ID as new execution

run_and_resource_lifecycle_mapping.basis: Current run states evidenced in src/control-plane/runs.js and src/runtime.js; normalized mapping is phase-owned under D-006.

mappings.condition: No matching active run; spawn and readiness confirmed.

mappings.resource: running or ready with original runId/PID

mappings.condition: Declared port/process is external, unknown, or conflicting.
Verified-owned run stops and declared ports release.
Stop attempted but process/port final state is bounded as failed.
mappings.effects: confirmed or possible; reconcile required when possible

mappings.condition: Old run stopped and new run ready.
Old run stopped but new start fails definitively.

mappings.journal: succeeded for zero exit, failed for nonzero, indeterminate on lost exit evidence
mappings.effects: per declared effect model; never inferred from exit code alone when mutations are possible

mappings.journal: original becomes reconciled; reconcile invocation succeeds as observation/recovery
mappings.outcome: original resolvedOutcome kept separate from current resource status

component_compatibility.basis: Spec-owned by compatibility rules and D-005/D-010; evaluation ordering is phase-owned.

transitions.guard: All required version ranges and digests match and state schemas are readable/writable for requested operation.
Identity can be read but a write axis is incompatible or digest mismatch exists.
Required protocol/state/manifest cannot be safely interpreted.
Runtime/Skill/schema digest drift, unsupported state version, or build mismatch is observed.
Explicit compatible update/migration is installed and exact identities revalidate.

component_compatibility.mutation_rule: Only compatible permits mutation; diagnosis_only/incompatible/unknown fail writes closed while returning bounded capabilities/diagnosis where readable.
component_compatibility.lifetime_rule: Plugin host exit, disable, update, or uninstall changes component presence only; it never transitions managed runs or deletes shared state.

skill_installation.basis: Specified by skill_migration_contract; content manifest algorithm and scan ordering are phase-owned.

transitions.guard: New install writes canonical content to host-preferred location only.
Observed target digest is identical; install/update is idempotent.
Second scanned location has the same canonical content digest.
Any scanned copy has a distinct content digest or incompatible build coupling.
User explicitly removes one identical duplicate; tool never deletes automatically.
User explicitly selects/migrates/removes content and a fresh scan proves convergence.

skill_installation.selection: For Codex identical duplicates, $HOME/.agents/skills wins over ~/.codex/skills. Divergent duplicates have no silent active selection/readiness claim.
prohibited: Automatic overwrite of divergent user content
Treating a Plugin-bundled copy as interchangeable with a user copy without build/digest reporting

transitions.guard: Exact cell scenario executes or a documented environment limitation is captured.
Build identity, relevant component digest/schema, host version, OS contract, or scenario implementation changes.

readiness_cell.rollup: A composite readiness label is passed only when every explicitly required exact cell is current and passed; otherwise it reports the narrow missing/failed/blocked/unsupported dimensions.

persistence_layout_recommendation.basis: Phase-owned design that extends the evidenced controlPlanePaths layout and atomic-write/lock patterns.

paths.change: reuse unchanged; ProjectScopeResolution reads this authority
reuse and evolve only through its own schema migration; journal links via runId

paths.authority: one atomic full OperationJournalRecord per mutation
paths.change: new; temp-file plus rename under operation lock

paths.authority: derived immutable creation index for bounded operation.list correlation
paths.change: new; append one redacted creation entry under operation-journal-index lock; record state is re-read from the authoritative record

paths.authority: optional bounded terminal Agent result blob when too large for record
paths.change: new; digest referenced by resultRef; no raw stdout/unbounded logs
new lock family using existing owner-aware lock record
reuse; add operationId to sanitized event correlation and expose only through cursor-bounded service

record_write_protocol: For new mutation, atomically persist prepared record and append its one creation-index entry before resource locks/effects.
Acquire resource locks in canonical order: operation, registry when needed, project, task, run-index/journal-index as short inner locks.
Re-evaluate scope/risk/ownership/compatibility/plan digest, atomically transition record, then execute.
Each later transition atomically replaces the operation record with revision+1; append sanitized lifecycle event for observability.
The date index is derived: missing/corrupt index can be rebuilt from records without inventing operation state; index disagreement never authorizes execution.

migrations_and_retention.basis: D-007/journal_contract plus existing fail-closed/migratedFrom patterns; compaction scheduling is phase-owned.
journal_schema: Initial journal schemaVersion is 1 under an explicit v1 directory.
Readers reject unsupported newer record versions for mutation and return bounded diagnostic metadata/path/version.
A future migration writes a new version atomically while preserving operationId/inputDigest/original provenance/effect evidence and records migratedFrom {schemaVersion,recordDigest,migratedAt}.
Unresolved prepared/running/indeterminate records are never destructively migrated without preserving raw evidence and a reversible diagnostic copy/reference.
Registry v2, run index v1, project runtime v1, lock v1, and event v1 keep independent versions; journal introduction does not rewrite them.

retention.terminal_clock: terminalAt; reconciled records count from reconciliation terminalAt.
retention.unresolved_rule: prepared, running, and indeterminate records have no age-based expiry and remain until explicit evidence-based reconciliation or separately specified safe administrative resolution.
retention.prune_rule: Compaction acquires the journal-index lock, verifies record is terminal and retainUntil is in the past, removes result/full record atomically, and rebuilds/prunes derived indexes. It never infers terminal state from age or dead PID alone.
retention.recovery_after_prune: Any supplied operation ID whose full record is absent returns operation_record_missing_or_expired and never creates new work, whether the ID never existed or was pruned.
retention.shared_state_rule: Plugin update/disable/uninstall never runs journal, run, registry, log, or event retention as a package-removal side effect.

skill_migration: Scan legacy/current Codex user locations and Plugin copies before install/update.
Identical copies are idempotent and non-destructive; current Codex user path has precedence.
Divergent copies remain untouched until explicit user-selected migration/removal, followed by a fresh digest scan.

The current test allowing --force overwrite is a verification/implementation gap against the new automatic non-destructive contract and must be partitioned or replaced by an explicitly reviewed migration operation, not exposed through public Agent inputs.

concurrency_and_idempotency.basis: Spec-owned by AC-003/AC-006/AC-007 and journal_contract; lock order extends live src/control-plane lock practice.
lock_order: run-index or operation-journal-index as short inner critical sections
rules: All Kernel paths use one canonical lock implementation; the current synchronous lifecycle-lock helpers and asynchronous control-plane locks must converge to avoid competing lock semantics.
A new mutation receives its Kernel ID and durable prepared record under the operation lock before any resource effect.
The operation lock remains outermost through execution/terminal transition; live owner PID blocks TTL takeover, and dead-owner takeover permits inspection/reconciliation only, not implicit resume.
Project/task locks protect run deduplication. After locks, start re-reads local/global run evidence; a matching active owned run returns original runId/PID and changed=false.
Same ID/same digest reads/reconciles the original record; running/indeterminate never invokes the handler again.
Same ID/different digest and missing/expired ID are hard refusals.
A new request without a recovery ID may create a new operation only after ordinary resource idempotency checks; this is not an exactly-once promise.
Restart records one operation identity across stop/start sub-effects and links old/new run IDs so partial completion is visible.

lost_response_recovery.known_id: Call operation.get/reconcile with the exact Kernel-issued ID.
lost_response_recovery.unknown_id: operation.list requires resolved projectRef, operationName, createdSince, one or more journal states, and taskRef or explicit null; default lookback 15 minutes, maximum lookback 30 retained days, default limit 50, maximum 200.
lost_response_recovery.ordering: createdAt descending then operationId descending under a snapshot high-water cursor.
lost_response_recovery.correlation: Exactly one candidate may be selected for subsequent get/reconcile. Zero returns operation_correlation_not_found; multiple returns operation_correlation_ambiguous with bounded summaries.
lost_response_recovery.safety: Neither list result authorizes retry or execution, and Skill fallback stops after uncertain mutation.

observability.basis: Spec-owned by CA-005/CA-006/CA-036/CA-042 and existing event redaction/limits.
events: Emit sanitized operation.prepared, operation.running, operation.succeeded, operation.failed, operation.refused, operation.indeterminate, and operation.reconciled events.

Each event carries operationId, transactionId equal to operationId unless an existing run transaction is separately linked, projectId, taskRef, runId, journal state, outcome code, effects certainty, build identity, and evidence refs.
Never persist request secrets, arbitrary environment values, full raw command output, or host protocol frames in journal/events.
diagnostics: Capabilities reports exact stateHome, schema versions, supported operations, risk boundary, host, project-scope modes, build/component digests, and compatibility status.
operation.get shows immutable identity, transition timestamps, current state, effects, resource refs, and provenance without raw unbounded observations.
Logs/events/operation.list use ObservationCursor bounds and return warnings for malformed/corrupt entries without treating partial reads as mutation authority.
Plugin/component and Skill duplicate observations remain visible after update/disable/uninstall via standalone CLI diagnosis when shared state is readable.

validation_routes.covers: OperationDefinition validation, canonical digest fixtures, journal transition table, effects certainty, AgentOperationResult schema, resource/outcome separation, same-ID rules, expired ID refusal.

Prepared-before-effects crash points, atomic replacement, corrupt/higher schema refusal, index rebuild, 30-day terminal pruning, indefinite unresolved retention, dead-lock owner takeover without replay.
validation_routes.evidence_owner: new journal state tests plus test/control-plane-state.test.js and test/control-plane-locks.test.js

validation_routes.covers: same-run reuse, start contention, unknown/external inspect-only, verified stop, stale mapping, restart partial state, cross-version CLI takeover.
validation_routes.evidence_owner: existing global-runtime/control-plane ownership tests plus new Kernel adapter-equivalence and fault tests

validation_routes.covers: missing/ambiguous/conflicting/unregistered roots, no Plugin-directory default, dynamic low-only MCP, forbidden inputs, component mismatch diagnosis-only and write refusal.
validation_routes.evidence_owner: new Kernel policy and MCP schema tests

validation_routes.covers: canonical digest fixture, fresh recomputation, drift refusal before deletion, all target safety cases, crash effects/reconcile, preserved run/failure evidence.
validation_routes.evidence_owner: test/clean-control-plane.test.js plus new Kernel clean digest tests

validation_routes.covers: cursor query binding, high-water snapshot, max limits/bytes, expiry, no arbitrary paths, no permanent follow, redaction, malformed lines.
validation_routes.evidence_owner: test/control-plane-events.test.js plus new logs/events/journal cursor tests

validation_routes.covers: current and legacy Codex scan, absent/single/identical/divergent/non-directory/unreadable, deterministic precedence, no automatic overwrite/delete, Plugin build coupling.
validation_routes.evidence_owner: test/agent-installer.test.js evolved for canonical digest contract and real host smoke

validation_routes.covers: cell identity, stale invalidation, exact host/OS/update/uninstall/recovery dimensions, roll-up refusal on any missing/non-pass cell.
validation_routes.evidence_owner: release evidence tooling and real Codex/Claude host smokes

architecture_impacts.impact: Introduce one Kernel-owned operation registry, context/policy pipeline, normalized result mapper, journal/recovery service, compatibility evaluator, and bounded observation service.
architecture_impacts.basis: Spec-owned by D-001/D-006/D-007; module paths are plan-owned.
architecture_impacts.risk: If CLI or MCP retains private mutation orchestration, safety and journal semantics diverge.
architecture_impacts.mitigation: Adapters resolve a declared operation and render results only; shared operation-contract and semantic-equivalence tests gate cutover.

architecture_impacts.impact: Extend controlPlanePaths with journal paths and reuse existing LAUNCHDECK_HOME, project/task/run-index locks, atomic JSON, events, and ownership evidence.
architecture_impacts.basis: Live-evidenced state owner; physical journal subpaths are phase-owned.
architecture_impacts.risk: Parallel helpers in src/runtime.js and src/control-plane modules currently expose more than one lock/run update path.
architecture_impacts.mitigation: Converge writes behind the control-plane owners during Kernel extraction; do not add a third Plugin/MCP state path.

architecture_impacts.impact: Compatibility manifest and component identities become prerequisites to mutation and readiness evidence.
architecture_impacts.risk: Conflated versions or path-based trust can allow unsafe cross-version writes.
architecture_impacts.mitigation: Separate axes/digests, diagnosis-only mismatch state, and fail writes closed.

architecture_impacts.impact: Agent installer/doctor must model observations across current/legacy user paths and Plugin copies instead of treating one explicit target as sufficient.
architecture_impacts.basis: Spec-owned by D-008; current explicit target/idempotency behavior is live-tested.
architecture_impacts.risk: Forced overwrite can destroy divergent user content or hide two active Skills.
architecture_impacts.mitigation: Canonical manifest digest, no silent winner for divergent copies, explicit migration route, and post-action rescan.

architecture_impacts.impact: Release status becomes a persisted/produced matrix of exact evidence cells, not one aggregate boolean.
architecture_impacts.risk: CLI/MCP unit success can be misreported as host/update/uninstall/platform readiness.

CA_refs.coverage_statement: CA-001 through CA-050 are all represented. CA-018/CA-019 remain deferred boundaries: no current medium-risk execution entity/state is activated. CA-030 is represented only as clean/adoption-plan digest safety; adoption.inspect never transitions to apply/start.

coverage_gaps.gap: No operation journal, canonical input digest fixture, result schema implementation, or retention/index compactor exists in the inspected live state modules.
coverage_gaps.basis: Phase-owned implementation gap; current live files expose registry/run/event state only.

coverage_gaps.gap: Current event/log observations are limit-bounded but do not expose stable high-water cursors; tailLog reads the full file before slicing.
Current synchronous lifecycle locks and asynchronous control-plane locks have different metadata/takeover behavior, so Kernel cutover must establish one mutation lock owner.

Current installer verification covers explicit targets and permits a forced divergent overwrite; it does not yet prove dual Codex-location digest classification or non-destructive divergent migration.
coverage_gaps.basis: Live evidence test/agent-installer.test.js:7-181 plus locked skill_migration_contract.

coverage_gaps.gap: ReadinessEvidenceCell storage/reporting and exact host/OS/update/uninstall/recovery receipts do not exist in the inspected state owners.
coverage_gaps.basis: Spec-owned required evidence, implementation phase gap.

integration_targets.target: Plan-level Application Kernel and Operation Registry modules
integration_targets.need: Own OperationDefinition, OperationRequest/Context, RiskDecision, AgentOperationResult, and all mutations.
integration_targets.basis: Spec-owned; final paths to be selected by leader architecture synthesis.

integration_targets.need: Remain LAUNCHDECK_HOME path/version authority and expose journal path helpers without Plugin-local alternatives.
Remain run/project-local resource truth while moving mutation entry behind Kernel and converging duplicate lock/run-index write helpers.
Produce OwnershipEvidence; Kernel never reimplements ownership heuristics.
Add operation correlation and bounded cursor service while preserving redaction and existing event/log authorities.
integration_targets.basis: Live-evidenced owner plus phase-owned service boundary.

integration_targets.target: Agent installer/doctor and Plugin packaging plan
integration_targets.need: Produce SkillInstallationObservation and ComponentIdentity from one CompatibilityManifest with non-destructive duplicate handling.
integration_targets.basis: Spec-owned contract; current verification owner identified.

integration_targets.need: Consume the same Kernel result and journal recovery API; CLI maps to schemaVersion 1, MCP returns typed AgentOperationResult.
Emit exact ReadinessEvidenceCell records and prevent unsupported roll-up.
integration_targets.basis: Spec-owned; final artifact placement to be selected by leader.
